title: lowRISC three-way Wishbone bus deadlock
tagline: A 3-master bus that can hang forever — proven by formal liveness.
hook_30s: Three masters share a Wishbone bus. The arbiter only hands the bus to the next master when the current one lets go. But if master A waits on B, B on C, and C on A — a circular wait — nobody ever lets go, and the bus hangs forever. ChipGPT proves it with a formal liveness check.

proof.note: Formal (bounded BMC, z3): a starvation counter shows a requesting master goes un-granted for the full bound — a concrete deadlock trace. A directed sim confirms the hang via watchdog timeout. (Unbounded liveness would use a liveness engine like suprove; commercial formal proves it directly.)

steps.agent: Task: check the shared Wishbone arbiter for liveness — can a requesting master be starved or deadlocked?
Reading the Wishbone arbitration rule. The arbiter must guarantee a pending master is eventually granted — a liveness property, not just a safety one.
Key risk per spec: a circular wait — each master holds the bus pending the next — deadlocks the fabric. CWE-833.
Parsing wb_arbiter.sv. Extracting the ownership FSM (owner_q) and the rotation condition.
Ownership only advances inside this guard.

DEADLOCK. The arbiter rotates ONLY when the current owner negates cyc_i (line 18). There is no timeout and no preemption. If owners 0->1->2->0 form a circular wait, none ever drops cyc_i, so owner_q never changes and no master is granted. The bus hangs forever.
Generating a liveness assertion and checking it formally.

artifact.explanation: A requesting master must be granted within a bound. Checked as bounded BMC via a starvation counter (no unbounded-liveness engine in the OSS flow).
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (BMC, z3): counterexample — master 2 starved for the full bound N (concrete deadlock trace)

steps.agent: Directed test: drive a 3-master circular wait, run with a watchdog.

artifact.name: t_wb_threeway_deadlock
artifact.stimulus: cyc_i = 3'b111 held; each master gated on the next's response.
artifact.expected: every master granted within N cycles
artifact.result_on_buggy: FAIL — watchdog fires; gnt_o stuck, no master ever granted
artifact.result_on_fixed: PASS — fairness/watchdog rotation grants each master in turn

artifact.title: Three-way Wishbone bus deadlock
artifact.root_cause: Arbiter rotates only when the current owner releases cyc_i; a circular wait means it never rotates. No timeout/preemption.
artifact.fix: Rotate ownership on a fairness/watchdog counter so a held grant cannot stall the fabric forever.
artifact.impact: The shared bus hangs; every master starves. Classic multi-master fabric deadlock.
artifact.why_agent_wins: Caught by bounded formal model checking — a concrete deadlock trace plus a watchdog sim — the class of bug random tests rarely hit.
